briefing.headline: Cover water — winter muddy conditions reward moving baits that trigger reaction strikes.
briefing.gameplan:
In winter with muddy water and stable conditions, moving baits presentations shine.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better act
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramou
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: In late February/early March, bass group up in underwater ditches 30-40 feet deep; targets them with underspin or small swimbait on light fluorocarbon.

KVD: In clear deep lakes with shad kills, fish often suspend. The higher they are in the water column, the more active they are. Fish off main lake points, humps, drops, centers of big bays — not near the bank. Alabama rig with spinners (keeps rig...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
